summary: DeVault praises PostgreSQL as one of the most significant achievements in free software, placing it alongside Linux and Firefox. He argues that after 25 years of development, Postgres has solved the relational database problem so effectively that its competitors are essentially obsolete. He highlights its powerful SQL implementation, exceptionally thorough documentation, and the disciplined engineering team behind it. While acknowledging that Postgres requires skilled operators and a costly learning investment, he concludes that the investment pays handsomely and that PostgreSQL deserves recognition as one of the most impactful projects in the free software ecosystem.
key_points: PostgreSQL has solved the relational database problem so thoroughly that all competitors except SQLite are essentially obsolete
The official PostgreSQL documentation is so comprehensive and in-depth that there is essentially no reason to consult blog posts or Stack Overflow for Postgres questions
The project's engineering discipline is legendary, exemplified by meticulous commit messages, detailed weekly updates, and thorough release notes
Despite being technically complex and requiring skilled operators, the investment in learning Postgres pays handsomely
money_quotes: PostgreSQL has taken a complex problem and solved it to such an effective degree that all of its competitors are essentially obsolete, perhaps with the exception of SQLite.

After 25 years of persistence, and a better logo design, Postgres stands today as one of the most significant pillars of profound achievement in free software, alongside the likes of Linux and Firefox.
There's essentially no reason to ever read a blog post or Stack Overflow answer about how to do something with Postgres — the official docs cover every aspect of the system in great depth.
No qualifiers. It's straight-up the best implementation of SQL.
It puts the rest of us to shame, really.
When I think of projects which have made the most significant impacts on the free software ecosystem, and on the world at large, PostgreSQL has a place on that list.
Learning to use it is a costly investment, even if it pays handsomely.
key_insight: PostgreSQL represents what free software can achieve at its best: a technically superior, exhaustively documented, and meticulously maintained project that has effectively solved its problem domain over 25 years of disciplined engineering.
